pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 documentation on project file creation and environment setup scripts
CDC official Epi Info 7 documentation: 'Make PRJ File' guide confirms .mdb is the default Microsoft Access database format

CDC official Epi Info 7 documentation states supported formats are: Microsoft Access 2000-2003 (.mdb, default) OR SQL Server (PRJ file only). SQL Server Compact (.sdf) is NOT documented as a supported format.
Epi Info 7 Form Designer allows any valid form name; no restrictions found in documentation

pi_items.verified_value: Text/TextBox field type is supported in Epi Info 7
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 Form Designer documentation lists 'Text' as a basic field type

pi_items.verified_value: Number field type is supported in Epi Info 7
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 Form Designer documentation lists 'Number' as a field type with predefined value patterns

pi_items.verified_value: Legal Values field type is supported in Epi Info 7 with custom dropdown options
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 documentation on Legal Value Field: 'Legal Values field appears in the form as a drop-down list of values'

pi_items.verified_value: Date field type is supported in Epi Info 7
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 Form Designer documentation lists 'Date Field' as an available field type

pi_items.metadata_value: Nausea, Vomiting, Diarrhea, AbdCramps, Fever, AteChicken, AtePastaSalad, AteRolls, DrankPunch (Yes/No)
pi_items.verified_value: Yes/No field type is supported in Epi Info 7 and appears as a drop-down list
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 documentation: 'Yes-No field is used to collect data with only a Yes or No answer. The field appears as a drop-down list on the canvas.'

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic Epi Info 7 project named 'NorovirusOutbreak' with a form 'CaseInvestigation' containing 15 fields. The project files (.prj and .mdb) will be stored in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Epi Info 7\Projects\NorovirusOutbreak\. All specified field types (Text, Number, Date, Yes/No, and Legal Values) are documented as supported by Epi Info 7 Form Designer. Note: The metadata incorrectly claims a .sdf database file path; official Epi Info 7 documentation specifies only Microsoft Access (.mdb, default) or SQL Server (PRJ-only) databases are supported, not SQL Server Compact (.sdf).
